sections.heading: (1) These Regulations may be cited as the Data Protection
sections.summary: These Regulations may be cited as the Data Protection (Registration of Data Controllers and Data Processors) Regulations.
sections.source_quote: These Regulations may be cited as the Data Protection (Registration of Data Controllers and Data Processors) Regulations,

sections.heading: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires
sections.summary: Defines 'establishment' as statutory instruments, registration certificates, trust deeds, and other instruments establishing a body.

requirements.text: establishment

sections.source_quote: means the person appointed under section 6 of the Act; ... (a) a Statute, Charter or statutory instrument in which a body is established; (b) registration certificate; (c) trust deeds in which a trust has been established; and (d) other instruments by which a body is established including its governing and administrative structure.

sections.heading: (1) These Regulations provide for the procedure for
sections.summary: Regulations provide registration procedure for data controllers and processors under Act section 18, excluding civil registration entities.
sections.dtia_summary: Regulations provide registration procedure for data controllers and processors under Act section 18, excluding civil registration entities.

requirements.text: These Regulations provide for the procedure for registration of data controllers and data processors as provided under section 18 of the Act.
These Regulations shall not apply to civil registration entities specified under the Data Protection (Civil Registration) Regulations

sections.source_quote: These Regulations provide for the procedure for registration of data controllers and data processors as provided under section 18 of the Act. These Regulations shall not apply to civil registration entities specified under the Data Protection (Civil Registration) Regulations,

sections.heading: (1) Subject to regulation 13 (1), every data controller and data
sections.summary: Data controllers and processors must register with the Data Commissioner; data controllers may register as both; data processors acting outside instructions become controllers.
sections.dtia_summary: Data controllers and processors must register with the Data Commissioner; data controllers may register as both; data processors acting outside instructions become controllers.

requirements.text: Subject to regulation 13 (1), every data controller and data processor shall be required to register in accordance with the provisions of the Act and these Regulations.

For purposes of registration, a person shall register as a data controller, where the person determines the purpose and means for processing personal data; or a data processor, where the person processes personal data on behalf of the data controller but excludes employees of the data controller and has (i) a contractual relationship with the data controller; and (ii) no decision making power on the purpose and means of processing personal data.

Despite sub-regulation (2) (a), a data controller may apply for registration as both a data controller and a data processor with regards to any processing operations and shall be required to pay the requisite fees applicable for both a data controller and a data processor thereto.

Despite sub-regulation (2) (b), where a data processor processes personal data other than as instructed by the data controller, the data processor shall be considered to be a data controller in respect of that processing activity, for purposes of assessing liability.

sections.source_quote: Subject to regulation 13 (1), every data controller and data processor shall be required to register in accordance with the provisions of the Act and these Regulations. For purposes of registration, a person shall register as a data controller, where the person determines the purpose and means for processing personal data; or a data processor, where the person processes personal data on behalf of the data controller but excludes employees of the data controller and has (i) a contractual relationship with the data controller; and (ii) no decision making power on the purpose and means of processing personal data.

sections.heading: (1) An application for registration of a data controller or data
sections.summary: Applications for registration must be in Form DPR1 and include registration fees, establishment documents, and details of data processing.
sections.dtia_summary: Applications for registration must be in Form DPR1 and include registration fees, establishment documents, and details of data processing.

requirements.text: An application for registration of a data controller or data processor shall be in Form DPR1 set out in the First Schedule; and be accompanied by the registration fees specified in the Second Schedule.

An application for registration under sub-regulation (1) shall be accompanied by a copy of the establishment documents; particulars of the data controllers or data processors including name and contact details; a description of the purpose for which personal data is processed; and a description of categories of personal data being processed.

sections.source_quote: An application for registration of a data controller or data processor shall (a) be in Form DPR1 set out in the First Schedule; and (b) be accompanied by the registration fees specified in the Second Schedule.

sections.heading: (1) A state department or county department shall register and
sections.summary: State and county departments register public entities on behalf of their entities; public entities must register as data controllers or processors.
sections.dtia_summary: State and county departments register public entities on behalf of their entities; public entities must register as data controllers or processors.

requirements.text: A state department or county department shall register and pay the fees on behalf of their respective entities.

The entities referred to under sub-regulation (1) shall be the public entities at national or county government which (a) operates within a state department or county department; (b) is wholly funded from the Consolidated Fund; and (c) provides a public service.
The fees paid by the state department or county department under sub-regulation (1) shall cater for the specified entities registered under the concerned state department or county department.

Despite this regulation, a State Corporation or a County Corporation shall be required to register as a data controller or a data processor in respect of their processing activity, in the manner specified under these Regulations.

sections.source_quote: A state department or county department shall register and pay the fees on behalf of their respective entities.

sections.heading: Where the Data Commissioner is satisfied that the applicant
sections.summary: Data Commissioner issues certificate of registration within 14 days if applicant meets requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: Data Commissioner issues certificate of registration within 14 days if applicant meets requirements.

requirements.text: Where the Data Commissioner is satisfied that the applicant fulfills the requirements for registration under these Regulations, the Data Commissioner shall, within fourteen days (a) issue the applicant with a certificate of registration for the duration specified under regulation 9; and (b) enter the particulars of the successful applicant in the register.

sections.source_quote: Where the Data Commissioner is satisfied that the applicant fulfills the requirements for registration under these Regulations, the Data Commissioner shall, within fourteen days (a) issue the applicant with a certificate of registration for the duration specified under regulation 9; and (b) enter the particulars of the successful applicant in the register.

sections.heading: A certificate of registration issued under regulation 8 (a) shall
sections.summary: Certificate of registration is valid for 24 months from issuance date.
sections.dtia_summary: Certificate of registration is valid for 24 months from issuance date.

requirements.text: A certificate of registration issued under regulation 8 (a) shall be valid for a period of twenty-four months from the date of issuance.

sections.source_quote: A certificate of registration issued under regulation 8 (a) shall be valid for a period of twenty-four months from the date of issuance.

sections.heading: (1) Where the Data Commissioner declines to approve an
sections.summary: Data Commissioner notifies applicants of refusal within 21 days with reasons; applicants may reapply after compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: Data Commissioner notifies applicants of refusal within 21 days with reasons; applicants may reapply after compliance.

requirements.text: Where the Data Commissioner declines to approve an application for registration, the Data Commissioner shall within twenty-one days from the date of such decision (a) notify, in writing, the applicant of the refusal; and (b) provide reasons for such refusal.

The Data Commissioner may decline to grant an application for registration, where the (a) particulars provided for inclusion in an entry in the register are insufficient; (b) appropriate safeguards for the protection of the privacy of the data subject have not been provided by the data controller or a data processor; or (c) the data controller or data processor is in violation of any provisions of the Act and these Regulations.

A data controller or data processor whose application for registration has been declined under these Regulations may make a fresh application upon complying with the requirements specified in the refusal notice.

sections.source_quote: Where the Data Commissioner declines to approve an application for registration, the Data Commissioner shall within twenty-one days from the date of such decision (a) notify, in writing, the applicant of the refusal; and (b) provide reasons for such refusal.

sections.heading: (1) Pursuant to section 20 of the Act, a registered data
sections.summary: Registered data controllers/processors must renew registration after certificate expiry; renewal requires form PR2 and renewal fees.
sections.dtia_summary: Registered data controllers/processors must renew registration after certificate expiry; renewal requires form PR2 and renewal fees.

requirements.text: Pursuant to section 20 of the Act, a registered data controller or data processor shall apply for a renewal of registration as a data controller or data processor, after the expiry of the certificate of registration.

An application for renewal of a certificate of registration shall be (a) made in Form PR 2 set out in the First Schedule; and (b) accompanied by the appropriate renewal fee specified in the Second Schedule.

sections.source_quote: Pursuant to section 20 of the Act, a registered data controller or data processor shall apply for a renewal of registration as a data controller or data processor, after the expiry of the certificate of registration.

sections.heading: (1) Where the Data Commissioner declines to renew an
sections.summary: Data Commissioner notifies applicants of refusal to renew within 21 days with reasons; regulation 10 applies to renewals.
sections.dtia_summary: Data Commissioner notifies applicants of refusal to renew within 21 days with reasons; regulation 10 applies to renewals.

requirements.text: Where the Data Commissioner declines to renew an application, the Data Commissioner shall within twenty-one days from the date of such decision (a) notify, in writing, the applicant of the refusal; and (b) provide reasons for such refusal.
Regulation 10 applies to renewals.

sections.source_quote: Where the Data Commissioner declines to renew an application, the Data Commissioner shall within twenty-one days from the date of such decision (a) notify, in writing, the applicant of the refusal; and (b) provide reasons for such refusal.

sections.heading: (1) For purposes of this regulation
sections.summary: Defines thresholds for mandatory registration based on number of employees and annual turnover.

requirements.text: A data controller or data processor processing personal data for the following purposes shall register as a data controller or a data processor as provided for under these Regulations.

sections.source_quote: A data controller or data processor processing personal data for the following purposes shall register as a data controller or a data processor as provided for under these Regulations
